description:
Spike output from mutagen-wrd.19. **Option B disposition.**

Advances: mutagen.coverage, mutagen.mutation_pipeline

## Root cause (confirmed by mutagen-wrd.19 spike)

End-to-end Scenario 7 (`:ecto_user_scenario`) has been `@tag :skip` since
`.13` under the framing "cover-instrumentation issue with Ecto-style
DSLs". That framing is wrong.

Direct reproduction (`priv/helper_scripts/spike_19_repro.exs`, landed in
.19) showed every macro-injected callback on `LaneFixture.EctoUser`
(`__schema_kind__/0`, `field/2`-generated `name/0` / `age/0`, `birthday/1`,
and the persisted `:lane_schema_kind` attribute) survives the full
`:cover.compile_beam/1` → `:cover.stop/0` → `:code.purge/1` →
`:code.load_file/1` cycle byte-for-byte. Module `__info__(:md5)` returns
to the disk-baseline value after `:cover.stop/0`.

The baseline-red (`failed: 1, passed: 5`) comes from a single assertion
in the FIXTURE test:

```elixir
# test/fixtures/lane_project/test/lane_fixture/ecto_user_test.exs:30
test "persisted attribute survives compile (tight)" do
  attrs = LaneFixture.EctoUser.__info__(:attributes)
  assert Keyword.has_key?(attrs, :lane_schema_kind)
  assert :registered in Keyword.get_values(attrs, :lane_schema_kind)
end
```

`Module.register_attribute(__MODULE__, :lane_schema_kind, persist: true)`
without `accumulate: true` produces `attrs == [..., lane_schema_kind: [:registered]]`
— the attribute value is itself a list (Erlang attribute format wraps it
before serialising into the BEAM attributes chunk). `Keyword.get_values/2`
then returns `[[:registered]]`, and `:registered in [[:registered]]` is
`false`.

The test fails identically before, during, and after `:cover.compile_beam/1`.
There is no `:cover` interaction — the fixture-test assertion is just
wrong.

## Fix

1. Fix the assertion in
   `test/fixtures/lane_project/test/lane_fixture/ecto_user_test.exs`:

   ```elixir
   # Either flatten:
   assert :registered in List.flatten(Keyword.get_values(attrs, :lane_schema_kind))

   # Or fetch the single value list:
   assert Keyword.fetch!(attrs, :lane_schema_kind) == [:registered]
   ```

2. Un-skip Scenario 7 in `test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs:789` by
   removing `@tag :skip` and replace the now-stale framing block-comment
   (lines 776-787) with a brief pointer to mutagen-wrd.19's findings.

3. Confirm `mix test --only ecto_user_scenario` is green and the bytecode-
   identical-MD5 check at the end of the scenario passes.

4. Update README "Known limitations" item 5 to mark Scenario 7
   un-skipped (the entry already references this ticket).

5. CHANGELOG: move the .19 investigation note from "Investigation notes"
   to "Fixed", referencing this ticket.

## Allowed touches

- `test/fixtures/lane_project/test/lane_fixture/ecto_user_test.exs` (the assertion fix)
- `test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs` (un-skip + comment update; minimal scope)
- `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` (caveat language)

## Out of Scope

- `lib/` source. The production code path is correct — this is purely
  a fixture-test bug.
- Refactoring the `LaneFixture.EctoUserSchema` macro DSL. The macro is
  fine; the test that probes it was just written incorrectly.

## Verification

- `mix test --only ecto_user_scenario` is green
- The Spike-I bytecode-identical-restore MD5 check passes for `LaneFixture.EctoUser`
- README + CHANGELOG reflect the un-skip

- MutagenEx.EndToEndTest Scenario 7 "after full mutation pass, source and bytecode are
  identical (r6, r11)" — `@tag :skip` removed; bytecode-identical-MD5 invariant now
  exercised end-to-end

Verification stubs flipped: none (this ticket has no spec-verification stubs; it's a
fixture-test fix + un-skip + doc cleanup advancing mutagen.coverage and
mutagen.mutation_pipeline by exercising the existing r6/r11 contracts against the
EctoUser DSL fixture).

Audit-expanded scope:
- `.spec/decisions/timeout_handling.md` — outside the ticket's Allowed touches
  (test/, README.md, CHANGELOG.md). Edited per the orchestrator's worker-prompt
  directive: "Also correct the disproved framing in .spec/decisions/timeout_handling.md
  per .19's spike findings." The Consequences -> Positive section originally claimed
  the Code.Server load-lock release unblocks both Scenario 4 (`infinite_looper.ex`)
  AND Scenario 7 (`EctoUser` bytecode-identical restore). The .19 spike disproved
  the Scenario 7 half — Scenario 7 is unblocked by the fixture-test assertion fix,
  not by this decision. The Scenario 4 attribution and the two-phase cancel +
  `:code.purge/1` settle rationale are unchanged.

Deferred must requirements: none.

Verification ran in stage worktree:
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: clean
- `mix test --only ecto_user_scenario`: 1 test, 0 failures
  (Scenario 7 passes; post-pipeline `assert post_md5 == pre_md5` at
  end_to_end_test.exs:819-821 passes)
- `mix test` (default suite, e2e_slow excluded): 362 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded)

Note (informational, not blocking): `mix test --include e2e_slow` shows a
pre-existing Scenario 4 (`infinite_looper_scenario`) golden drift in
`test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_4_infinite_looper.json`. Confirmed
present before any change in this ticket (stash test). Not related to .32 — flagging
for the orchestrator's awareness.

Summary: Fixed the list-of-lists membership bug in the fixture-test assertion
(`Module.register_attribute/3` with `persist: true` wraps values in a list before
serialising into the BEAM attributes chunk, so `Keyword.get_values/2` returns
`[[:registered]]`). Removed `@tag :skip` from end-to-end Scenario 7 so the
Spike-I bytecode-identical-restore invariant is now exercised against the
hand-rolled DSL. Updated README, CHANGELOG, and the disproved-framing portion of
.spec/decisions/timeout_handling.md to match the .19 spike's findings.

title: FIX: Scenario 7 (:ecto_user_scenario) un-skip — fixture-test persisted-attribute assertion bug
